codeReview.summary: The feature lands the core Files/Uploads handles and good mocked coverage, but it misses two contract-critical details: Files multipart `expires_after` is encoded incorrectly, and the Uploads typed surface advertises purposes that the current Uploads API does not accept. I also noted one forward-compatibility gap in upload status parsing.

issues.description: `Files::create` serializes `expires_after` as a single multipart text field containing JSON (`expires_after: {"anchor":...,"seconds":...}`), but the current Files API contract requires multipart fields `expires_after[anchor]` and `expires_after[seconds]`. The companion test locks in the same wrong wire format (`tests/files_contract_mock.rs:50-53`), and the live smoke never exercises `expires_after`, so this bug would ship unnoticed for any caller using expiration.

`UploadCreateParams` and `UploadChunkedParams` reuse `FilePurpose`, which exposes values such as `user_data` and `evals`, but the current Uploads API only documents `assistants`, `batch`, `fine-tune`, and `vision` for upload creation. The tests even assert `FilePurpose::UserData` through the chunked helper (`tests/upload_helpers_contract.rs:53`), so the typed surface is encouraging a documented-invalid request shape that will lead callers into avoidable 4xx failures.

`UploadStatus` has no `Unknown`/`#[serde(other)]` fallback, unlike existing status enums in `src/resources/vector_stores.rs` (for example lines 827-855). Any additive server-side upload status would therefore turn an otherwise successful response into a parse error, which conflicts with the architecture's forward-compatibility invariant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library docs do not capture an important Files-vs-Uploads encoding asymmetry: Files multipart `expires_after` uses bracketed form fields, while Uploads create accepts a JSON object. That gap made it easy for this worker to codify the wrong multipart shape and for tests to bless it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/api-surface.md` only lists Files/Uploads at the family level. Current API reference at `https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/files/methods/create/` shows `-F expires_after[anchor]=...` and `-F expires_after[seconds]=...`, while `src/resources/files.rs:187-190` sends a single JSON-string field and `tests/files_contract_mock.rs:50-53` assert that exact shape.

summary: Review failed. The feature broadly implements the requested Files/Uploads surface, but `files.create` emits the wrong multipart contract for `expires_after`, and the Uploads public typing overstates which purposes are valid. I also found a smaller forward-compatibility issue in upload status parsing.
